records.ety: [OE. sterre, AS. steorra; akin to OFries. stera, OS. sterro, D. ster, OHG. sterno, sterro, G. stern, Icel. stjarna, Sw. stjerna, Dan. stierne, Goth. starnō, Armor. & Corn. steren, L. stella, Gr. 'asth`r, 'a`stron, Skr. star; perhaps from a root meaning, to scatter, Skr. st, L. sternere (cf. Stratum), and originally applied to the stars as being strewn over the sky, or as being scatterers or spreaders of light. √296. Cf. Aster, Asteroid, Constellation, Disaster, Stellar.]

defs: One of the innumerable luminous bodies seen in the heavens; any heavenly body other than the sun, moon, comets, and nebulae.
quotes: His eyen twinkled in his head aright, As do the stars in the frosty night.
notes: ☞ The stars are distinguished as planets, and fixed stars. See Planet, Fixed stars under Fixed, and Magnitude of a star under Magnitude.

defs: A planet supposed to influence one's destiny; (usually pl.) a configuration of the planets, supposed to influence fortune.
quotes: Blesses his stars, and thinks it luxury.

defs: That which resembles the figure of a star, as an ornament worn on the breast to indicate rank or honor.
quotes: On whom . . . Lavish Honor showered all her stars.

defs: Specifically, a radiated mark in writing or printing; an asterisk [thus, *]; -- used as a reference to a note, or to fill a blank where something is omitted, etc.
A composition of combustible matter used in the heading of rockets, in mines, etc., which, exploding in the air, presents a starlike appearance.
A person of brilliant and attractive qualities, especially on public occasions, as a distinguished orator, a leading theatrical performer, etc.
quotes: With the old flag, the true American flag, the Eagle, and the Stars and Stripes, waving over the chamber in which we sit.
notes: ☞ Star is used in the formation of compound words generally of obvious signification; as, star-aspiring, star-bespangled, star-bestudded, star-blasting, star-bright, star-crowned, star-directed, star-eyed, star-headed, star-paved, star-roofed, star-sprinkled, star-wreathed.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Starred (stärd); p. pr. & vb. n. Starring.]

defs: To set or adorn with stars, or bright, radiating bodies; to bespangle; as, a robe starred with gems.
To be bright, or attract attention, as a star; to shine like a star; to be brilliant or prominent; to play a part as a theatrical star.
